ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03302051 represents chicken wing food product as a closest parent; no specific fried/crispy Malaysian chicken wing term exists in FoodOn.
ontology.foodex2_description: Chicken meat — fried preparation (closest parent category for fried chicken products)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC used as closest parent for fried chicken; no specific code for Malaysian-style crispy fried chicken wings exists in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226529007 (Fried chicken) used as closest parent; no specific code for crispy fried chicken wings in SNOMED CT.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of crispy fried chicken wings at a Malaysian coffee shop or restaurant consists of approximately 3 to 4 wings, weighing around 200 g in total.

nutrient_source.title: Fried chicken wing, with skin — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB No. 05062)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy fried chicken wing with skin used as the primary nutrient reference. Values are adapted to represent a Malaysian-style deep-fried chicken wing with batter coating; sodium adjusted upward slightly to reflect typical Malaysian seasoning. IMR Malaysia MyFCD does not publish a specific fried chicken wing entry with full macro and micronutrient panel; USDA SR Legacy is the highest-quality available approved source for this preparation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of mixed fried meat dishes — analogous estimate based on fried chicken (GI ~45) from University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Malaysian crispy fried chicken wings. GI estimated at 45 based on fried chicken entries in the University of Sydney GI Database. Fried chicken with batter coating is a high-protein, moderate-fat food with minimal carbohydrate content; the low GI reflects the dominant protein and fat matrix suppressing glycemic response. GL per serving calculated as (3.8 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 200 g serving / 100) × 45 / 100 = 3.4.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~480 mg per 100 g; ~960 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 1–2 wings per meal, as a standard 200 g serving provides approximately 960 mg sodium, representing nearly 42% of the 2300 mg daily recommended limit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (GI ~45) but high saturated fat and total fat content
type2_diabetes.guidance: While the low GI of fried chicken wings poses minimal acute glycemic risk, the high total fat (18.5 g/100 g) and saturated fat (5.1 g/100 g) content may contribute to insulin resistance over time; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this food occasionally and in controlled portions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat, saturated fat, cholesterol, and sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: A 200 g serving provides approximately 37 g total fat, 10.2 g saturated fat, and 176 mg cholesterol alongside ~960 mg sodium; individuals with cardiovascular disease should restrict consumption of deep-fried chicken wings and consider grilled or baked alternatives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Crispy fried chicken wings are a popular snack and side dish in Malaysian cuisine, commonly served at kopitiam (coffee shops), hawker stalls, and casual dining restaurants. The wings are typically marinated in a blend of garlic, salt, soy sauce, and spices, then coated in a seasoned wheat flour batter and deep-fried in hot oil until the skin is golden brown and exceptionally crispy. The flesh inside remains juicy and tender, while the exterior provides a satisfying crunch. In Singapore, this dish is widely available at Malaysian-style coffee shops such as EC Coffee Bar. Nutritionally, fried chicken wings are a high-protein food (approximately 27 g protein per 100 g) with significant fat content (approximately 18.5 g per 100 g), including saturated fat. The carbohydrate content is low (approximately 3.8 g per 100 g), resulting in a low glycemic index of approximately 45. The sodium content is elevated due to seasoning and batter, making portion control important for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The dish is not suitable for vegetarians or vegans and contains gluten from the batter coating. It is generally considered halal when prepared with halal-certified chicken and oil.

confidence_reason: The food name 'Crisp Harmony Wings' is a branded/menu name from a single restaurant (EC Coffee Bar, Singapore). The canonical food type is Malaysian-style crispy fried chicken wings. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy fried chicken wing with skin (NDB 05062), which is the closest approved-source match available. IMR Malaysia MyFCD does not publish a full nutrient panel for this specific preparation. Confidence is medium because the source is adapted rather than an exact match to the Malaysian restaurant preparation.
notes: 1. 'Crisp Harmony Wings' is a proprietary menu name used by EC Coffee Bar (Carpenter Street, Singapore); the canonical food identity is Malaysian-style crispy deep-fried chicken wings. 2. Nutrient values adapted from USDA SR Legacy fried chicken wing with skin; sodium adjusted to 480 mg/100 g to reflect typical Malaysian seasoning levels. 3. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for fried chicken wing; this is a confirmed zero as fried chicken contains negligible vitamin C. 4. GI estimated at 45 (low) based on analogous fried chicken entries in the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for Malaysian crispy fried chicken wings was found. 5. GL per 200 g serving = (3.8 × 200 / 100) × 45 / 100 = 3.42, rounded to 3.4. 6. trans_fat_g is 0.1 — small residual trans fat from deep-frying oil, consistent with USDA data. 7. FoodOn, FoodEx2, and SNOMED CT codes are closest_parent matches; no exact ontology code exists for Malaysian crispy fried chicken wings. 8. Halal status assumed based on typical Malaysian Muslim-majority food preparation context; individual restaurant certification should be verified. 9. Price SGD 26.30 preserved from input record (single restaurant, single price point). 10. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all reported from USDA SR Legacy source data for fried chicken wing with skin.
